subject: Upgrade to release oparent
commit_message:
Upgrade to release oparent

Following independent release and versioning process guidelines.

Removed 'parent' from 'drools-artifact1' pom.xml files --
hopefully, this will get around the build problems.

Issue-ID: POLICY-225
Change-Id: I27af13edd1a39daee09c8bca6ff726881144212e
Signed-off-by: Pamela Dragosh <pdragosh@research.att.com>

 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/200/ )

Patch Set 1:

Gary, can you help us with this? We were able to successfully move to release oparent in other projects. But this project seems to have an issue.

Patch Set 1:

Do you know why this project is trying to resolve oparent during test phase?  What repository settings are being used during the test phase?

Patch Set 1:

Yes in order to meet code coverage, our JUnit tests must utilize nexus repo that the drools controller uses to load rules for testing.

Patch Set 1:

Gary, can you suggest a way to move forward on this without losing the coverage we had in the junits that deal with maven artifacts?   Thank you

Patch Set 1:

Since the compile phase appears to resolve oparent fine, we need to figure out how your test code (org.kie.api.builder.KieScanner) is determining its repository settings, and log a request with LF to get them to pass these settings to Maven.

I'm not familiar with this KieScanner thing.  Any ideas?

Patch Set 1:

Jorge, Ralph - please address this today.

Patch Set 1:

How do I reproduce this problem, so I can look at in more detail? I tried to build exactly this commit, starting with an empty local repository, and the only Nexus repositories I referenced in 'settings.xml' were ONAP. The build was successful, and I looked at the '_maven.repositories' files in my local repository, and verified that every artifact came from one of these two repositories:

- https://nexus.onap.org/content/groups/public/
- https://nexus.onap.org/content/repositories/snapshots/

The vast majority came from 'public', with only these artifacts coming from 'snapshots'

- org.onap.oparent:version-check-maven-plugin:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
- org.onap.oparent:version:1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
- org.onap.policy.common:integrity-monitor:1.1.0-SNAPSHOT
- org.onap.policy.common:ONAP-Logging:1.1.0-SNAPSHOT
- org.onap.policy.common:common-modules:1.1.0-SNAPSHOT

What is different about this Gerrit build?

Patch Set 1:

fyi: ticket opened with helpdesk

Patch Set 1:

One thing that I did discover is the 'settings.xml' file used when running the Junit tests is not necessarily the same one used while compiling. If the '-s' option is used to pass a 'settings.xml' file to 'mvn', the Drools container could be dynamically loading artifacts and dependencies using a different 'settings.xml' file, presumably using the default rules. This may be related to what is happening here.

By the way, I also did another test, where I removed changed the 'drools-artifact1' pom.xml files by removing the 'parent' element, but leaving 'groupId'. That did compile correctly, and may be a workaround for this problem.

Patch Set 1:

Ralph, not sure about the possibility of providing a custom settings.xml.   

I think it is worth though to give submit it to gerrit for a quick try: removing the oparent section on the test artifacts but keeping in our root pom the release version change, to see if it passes the "verify", we could abandon that one if does not pass the verify.  

Is it something you may have time to do?
Uploaded patch set 2.
